description: In the Framework, Mack reassures his daughter Hope after the others have departed. He sits with her, emotionally deciding to remain in the simulated world rather than face the loss of his daughter in reality. This is an emotionally-charged resolution to Mack’s arc in the Framework, showcasing his unwillingness to leave behind what he loves, even if unreal.
location: Framework (virtual reality simulation / Dr. Radcliffe / Aida construct)

transcript.line: I didn't think you were coming back. Something go wrong?
No. No, they all made it through.
She doesn't know what happened. I was waiting till tomorrow to tell her, but I guess I don't have to now. Hey. Hey, look who's here.
Dad. I tried waiting up for you.
I'm sorry, Sparkplug. The, uh the mission was a little more complicated than expected.
Where's Daisy and the rest of your team?
Oh, yeah, yeah, they're all good. It's just... You know in “The Wizard of Oz,” at the end, how Dorothy goes to Kansas?
Yeah, well, that's where they went a place like Kansas.
Does that mean we're going to Kansas, too?
Nah. We're gonna stay right here. Everything I need it's all right here.
